label: Figma
description: Access Figma design files, prototypes, and components via the Figma REST API.

form.title: Connect Figma
form.subtitle: Paste your Figma personal access token to give Anton access to your design files and prototypes.

methods.label: Personal Access Token
methods.description: Generate a token in Figma Settings → Personal access tokens and paste it here. No OAuth setup required — the fastest way to connect.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a Figma Personal Access Token

Figma personal access tokens are scoped credentials you can create directly from your account settings. No admin access or OAuth app required.

### Steps

1. Open [Figma](https://www.figma.com) and log in.
2. Click your **avatar** in the top-left corner → **Settings**.
3. Scroll down to the **Personal access tokens** section.
4. Click **Generate new token**.
5. Give the token a descriptive name like `Anton`.
6. Choose an expiration (or set to **No expiration** for a long-lived token).
7. Click **Generate token**. Figma shows the token **once** — copy it immediately.
8. Paste the token into the **Personal Access Token** field below.

### Notes

- The token inherits your account's access level — it can read any file or project you can access.
- If you lose the token before pasting it, just delete it in Settings and generate a new one.
- To revoke access later, return to **Settings → Personal access tokens** and delete the `Anton` entry.
- Tokens are scoped to your personal account; for team-wide access consider using an org service account.

fields.name: personal_access_token
fields.label: Personal Access Token
fields.description: Found in Figma → Settings → Personal access tokens. The token is shown only once after generation.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0
methods.description: Use a Figma OAuth app (client ID + secret) to authorize via browser. Suitable for team deployments or when you prefer not to use a personal token.
methods.how_to:
## OAuth 2.0 via a Figma App

This method requires you to register an OAuth application in the Figma developer portal and supply the credentials below.

### Steps

1. Go to [Figma Developer Console](https://www.figma.com/developers/apps) and log in.
2. Click **New app** and fill in the app name (e.g. `Anton`) and a description.
3. Under **Redirect URLs**, add `http://localhost` (Anton uses a local redirect during the OAuth flow).
4. Select the **File content** scope (`file_read`) — this is the minimum needed.
5. Click **Save**. Figma shows your **Client ID** and **Client secret**.
6. Copy both values and paste them into the fields below.
7. Click **Submit** — your browser will open Figma's consent screen.
8. Approve the permissions and Anton will store the resulting tokens.

### Notes

- Figma OAuth tokens do not issue refresh tokens by default; you may need to re-authorize periodically.
- The personal access token method above is simpler for individual use.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From Figma Developer Console → your app → Client ID.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: From Figma Developer Console → your app → Client secret. Keep this private.
